model_narrative: We plan how Turkey's electricity system expands over a sequence of milestone years stretching to 2005. For each milestone year we decide how much new hydro and thermal generating capacity to build at each candidate plant, and for every load block of the year we decide how much power each existing and newly built plant should produce. Capacity once built stays available in all later years, and demand grows year over year. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of building and operating the system over the whole horizon as small as possible.

sets.name: time
sets.doc: every calendar year of the full planning span from 1975 to 2005, in chronological order; used to count how many years separate two dates, where the count is the position of the year within this span measured from the very first year

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the extended list of milestone years including the base year 1975, in chronological order

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the milestone decision years excluding the base year, in chronological order; these are the years at which capacity may be added and at which the system must be checked; a year counts as on-or-after another when it is at the same position or later in this ordered list

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the load blocks of the annual load-duration curve, listed from the highest-demand block to the lowest; 'peak' is the first block; a block is said to lie at-or-below another in this merit ordering when it appears at the same position or later in this list

sets.name: mh
sets.doc: the candidate hydro plants

sets.name: mt
sets.doc: the candidate thermal plant types

params.name: mdatah_initcap
params.doc: hydro plant capacity already in place at the start, in MW

params.name: mdatah_avail
params.doc: hydro plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatah_efact
params.doc: hydro plant energy factor, the fraction of installed capacity that bounds the plant's average sustained output over the year

params.name: mdatah_opcost
params.doc: hydro plant operating cost, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatah_capcost
params.doc: hydro plant capital cost, in million TL per MW of new capacity

params.name: mdatah_life
params.doc: hydro plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatah_maxcap
params.doc: the most new hydro capacity that may ever be built at a given hydro plant over the whole horizon, in MW

params.name: mdatat_initcap
params.doc: thermal capacity already in place at the start, in MW; this existing stock is treated as a vintage commissioned in the first decision year 1978

params.name: mdatat_avail
params.doc: thermal plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatat_opcost
params.doc: thermal operating cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatat_opcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal operating cost; the operating cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_capcost
params.doc: thermal capital cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW

params.name: mdatat_capcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal capital cost; the capital cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_life
params.doc: thermal plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatat_maxcap
params.doc: the most new thermal capacity that may be built for a given type, in MW; unbounded for types with no listed limit

params.name: dd_duration
params.doc: the number of hours per year that the system spends in each load block

params.name: dd_demand
params.doc: the power demand of each load block in the base year 1975, in MW; the demand of any later year is this base demand grown by the demand growth rate over the number of years from 1975, rounded to the nearest MW

params.name: rho
params.doc: the discount (interest) rate per year

params.name: prr
params.doc: the planning reserve margin, the fraction of peak demand that available capacity must exceed

params.name: r
params.doc: the largest share of peak demand that aggregate hydro capacity is allowed to cover

params.name: g
params.doc: the annual demand growth rate

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted system cost over the horizon, in million TL

vars.name: phic
vars.doc: the capital charges incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: phio
vars.doc: the operating costs incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: hh
vars.doc: new hydro capacity added at a given hydro plant in a given milestone year, in MW

vars.name: ht
vars.doc: new thermal capacity added for a given type in a given decision year, in MW; the year at which it is added is that vintage's commissioning year

vars.name: htt
vars.doc: total new thermal capacity built for a given type across the whole horizon, in MW

vars.name: zh
vars.doc: hydro power output of a given plant in a given load block in a given year, in MW

vars.name: zt
vars.doc: thermal power output indexed by type, then the vintage's commissioning year, then the load block, then the operating year, in MW; a vintage can only produce in years at or after its commissioning year

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every load block in every decision year, the total power produced across all generators must be enough to meet that block's demand. Because the load blocks are stacked from the heaviest block down, the production that serves a given block is the output of every plant in that block together with the output it carries in all the lighter blocks beneath it, and only generating vintages that have already been commissioned by that year may contribute. Demand for the year is the base-year demand of the block grown forward at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the system must hold enough firm capacity to cover peak demand with a reserve margin on top. Each plant's contribution is its availability fraction applied to the capacity it has in place that year, which is its starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, where the existing thermal stock counts as a first-year vintage. The sum of these firm contributions across all hydro and thermal plants must be at least the peak demand inflated by the reserve margin, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the plant cannot generate more than the capacity it actually has available. Adding up the plant's output over all load blocks in that year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times the capacity in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every thermal vintage in every decision year at or after the year that vintage was commissioned, the vintage cannot generate more than the capacity it has available. Adding up that vintage's output over all load blocks in the year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times its installed capacity, which is the amount built for that vintage plus, for the very first vintage only, the pre-existing thermal stock of that type.

constraints.name: t);
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the energy the plant delivers over the year is limited by its energy factor, not just its instantaneous capacity. Weighting the plant's output in each load block by the share of the year that the block and all lighter blocks below it occupy, the weighted total may not exceed the plant's energy factor times the capacity it has in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, hydro must not dominate the system. The combined capacity of all hydro plants in place that year, counting their starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, may not exceed an allowed share of that year's peak demand, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: hh[mhh,
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant, the total of all new capacity built at that plant across all decision years may not exceed the most new capacity that plant is ever allowed to add.

constraints.name: htt[mtt]
constraints.intent: For every thermal type, the recorded total new capacity equals the sum of the capacity additions made for that type across all decision years.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the capital charges of that year are defined as the annualized cost of all capacity standing that year. For each hydro plant, take its capital recovery factor times its capital cost applied to the capacity it has commissioned up to and including that year. For each thermal type, take its capital recovery factor applied to its additions, with each vintage's addition valued at that vintage's escalated capital cost, which is the base capital cost compounded at its capital cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year, counting only vintages commissioned up to and including that year. The capital recovery factor spreads a plant's capital cost over its economic life at the discount rate. Set the year's capital charges equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the operating costs of that year are defined from the energy each plant delivers. For each hydro plant, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at its operating cost. For each thermal vintage commissioned up to and including the year, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at that vintage's escalated operating cost, which is the base operating cost compounded at its operating cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year. The duration weight of a block is the share of the year occupied by that block together with all lighter blocks below it. Set the year's operating costs equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by bringing every year's costs back to present value. For each decision year, discount that year's capital charges and operating costs together by the discount factor for that year, which compounds the discount rate over the years from the base year. Set the total discounted cost equal to the sum of these discounted yearly costs over all decision years.

query: There's a proposal on the table to keep nuclear from dominating the fleet — the idea is that the total new nuclear capacity shouldn't account for more than 45% of all the new thermal capacity the plan installs across every unit type combined. What does the optimizer return for total cost once we hold nuclear to that share of the overall thermal build?
expected_pyomo: model.nuclear_share_cap = Constraint(expr=model.htt['nuclear'] <= 0.45 * sum(model.htt[mt] for mt in model.mt))

query: We're looking at a combined ceiling on the two big-ticket thermal programs. Suppose we say the new nuclear capacity and the new oil-fired capacity, added together over the whole horizon, can't exceed 65,000 MW between them. Plug that in and walk me through where total system cost lands.

query: Construction crews are stretched thin toward the end of the horizon. Say we capped all the new thermal capacity commissioned in the 2005 period combined at 45,000 MW — what total cost does the model come back with?
expected_pyomo: model.thermal_2005_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.ht[mt, 2005] for mt in model.mt) <= 45000)

sets.name: time
sets.doc: every calendar year of the full planning span from 1975 to 2005, in chronological order; used to count how many years separate two dates, where the count is the position of the year within this span measured from the very first year

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the extended list of milestone years including the base year 1975, in chronological order

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the milestone decision years excluding the base year, in chronological order; these are the years at which capacity may be added and at which the system must be checked; a year counts as on-or-after another when it is at the same position or later in this ordered list

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the load blocks of the annual load-duration curve, listed from the highest-demand block to the lowest; 'peak' is the first block; a block is said to lie at-or-below another in this merit ordering when it appears at the same position or later in this list

sets.name: mh
sets.doc: the candidate hydro plants

sets.name: mt
sets.doc: the candidate thermal plant types

params.name: mdatah_initcap
params.doc: hydro plant capacity already in place at the start, in MW

params.name: mdatah_avail
params.doc: hydro plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatah_efact
params.doc: hydro plant energy factor, the fraction of installed capacity that bounds the plant's average sustained output over the year

params.name: mdatah_opcost
params.doc: hydro plant operating cost, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatah_capcost
params.doc: hydro plant capital cost, in million TL per MW of new capacity

params.name: mdatah_life
params.doc: hydro plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatah_maxcap
params.doc: the most new hydro capacity that may ever be built at a given hydro plant over the whole horizon, in MW

params.name: mdatat_initcap
params.doc: thermal capacity already in place at the start, in MW; this existing stock is treated as a vintage commissioned in the first decision year 1978

params.name: mdatat_avail
params.doc: thermal plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatat_opcost
params.doc: thermal operating cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatat_opcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal operating cost; the operating cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_capcost
params.doc: thermal capital cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW

params.name: mdatat_capcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal capital cost; the capital cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_life
params.doc: thermal plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatat_maxcap
params.doc: the most new thermal capacity that may be built for a given type, in MW; unbounded for types with no listed limit

params.name: dd_duration
params.doc: the number of hours per year that the system spends in each load block

params.name: dd_demand
params.doc: the power demand of each load block in the base year 1975, in MW; the demand of any later year is this base demand grown by the demand growth rate over the number of years from 1975, rounded to the nearest MW

params.name: rho
params.doc: the discount (interest) rate per year

params.name: prr
params.doc: the planning reserve margin, the fraction of peak demand that available capacity must exceed

params.name: r
params.doc: the largest share of peak demand that aggregate hydro capacity is allowed to cover

params.name: g
params.doc: the annual demand growth rate

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted system cost over the horizon, in million TL

vars.name: phic
vars.doc: the capital charges incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: phio
vars.doc: the operating costs incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: hh
vars.doc: new hydro capacity added at a given hydro plant in a given milestone year, in MW

vars.name: ht
vars.doc: new thermal capacity added for a given type in a given decision year, in MW; the year at which it is added is that vintage's commissioning year

vars.name: htt
vars.doc: total new thermal capacity built for a given type across the whole horizon, in MW

vars.name: zh
vars.doc: hydro power output of a given plant in a given load block in a given year, in MW

vars.name: zt
vars.doc: thermal power output indexed by type, then the vintage's commissioning year, then the load block, then the operating year, in MW; a vintage can only produce in years at or after its commissioning year

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every load block in every decision year, the total power produced across all generators must be enough to meet that block's demand. Because the load blocks are stacked from the heaviest block down, the production that serves a given block is the output of every plant in that block together with the output it carries in all the lighter blocks beneath it, and only generating vintages that have already been commissioned by that year may contribute. Demand for the year is the base-year demand of the block grown forward at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the system must hold enough firm capacity to cover peak demand with a reserve margin on top. Each plant's contribution is its availability fraction applied to the capacity it has in place that year, which is its starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, where the existing thermal stock counts as a first-year vintage. The sum of these firm contributions across all hydro and thermal plants must be at least the peak demand inflated by the reserve margin, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the plant cannot generate more than the capacity it actually has available. Adding up the plant's output over all load blocks in that year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times the capacity in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every thermal vintage in every decision year at or after the year that vintage was commissioned, the vintage cannot generate more than the capacity it has available. Adding up that vintage's output over all load blocks in the year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times its installed capacity, which is the amount built for that vintage plus, for the very first vintage only, the pre-existing thermal stock of that type.

constraints.name: t);
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the energy the plant delivers over the year is limited by its energy factor, not just its instantaneous capacity. Weighting the plant's output in each load block by the share of the year that the block and all lighter blocks below it occupy, the weighted total may not exceed the plant's energy factor times the capacity it has in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, hydro must not dominate the system. The combined capacity of all hydro plants in place that year, counting their starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, may not exceed an allowed share of that year's peak demand, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: hh[mhh,
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant, the total of all new capacity built at that plant across all decision years may not exceed the most new capacity that plant is ever allowed to add.

constraints.name: htt[mtt]
constraints.intent: For every thermal type, the recorded total new capacity equals the sum of the capacity additions made for that type across all decision years.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the capital charges of that year are defined as the annualized cost of all capacity standing that year. For each hydro plant, take its capital recovery factor times its capital cost applied to the capacity it has commissioned up to and including that year. For each thermal type, take its capital recovery factor applied to its additions, with each vintage's addition valued at that vintage's escalated capital cost, which is the base capital cost compounded at its capital cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year, counting only vintages commissioned up to and including that year. The capital recovery factor spreads a plant's capital cost over its economic life at the discount rate. Set the year's capital charges equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the operating costs of that year are defined from the energy each plant delivers. For each hydro plant, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at its operating cost. For each thermal vintage commissioned up to and including the year, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at that vintage's escalated operating cost, which is the base operating cost compounded at its operating cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year. The duration weight of a block is the share of the year occupied by that block together with all lighter blocks below it. Set the year's operating costs equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by bringing every year's costs back to present value. For each decision year, discount that year's capital charges and operating costs together by the discount factor for that year, which compounds the discount rate over the years from the base year. Set the total discounted cost equal to the sum of these discounted yearly costs over all decision years.

query: It bugs me that the plan ends up with far more new nuclear capacity than new oil-fired capacity, when nuclear is the one with all the lead-time and siting headaches. Shouldn't the total new nuclear build come in no higher than the total new oil build, rather than overshooting it the way it does? What's the model seeing that makes it tip so heavily toward nuclear?

model_narrative: We plan how Turkey's electricity system expands over a sequence of milestone years stretching to 2005. For each milestone year we decide how much new hydro and thermal generating capacity to build at each candidate plant, and for every load block of the year we decide how much power each existing and newly built plant should produce. Capacity once built stays available in all later years, and demand grows year over year. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of building and operating the system over the whole horizon as small as possible.

query: The whole back end of the plan loads up on capacity in the final 2005 round, and the nuclear and oil additions in that one period are the bulk of it. Couldn't we hold the combined new nuclear-and-oil capacity that gets commissioned in 2005 to 35,000 MW and pull some of that build forward? What's keeping the model from spreading it out on its own?

query: The plan dumps huge slugs of nuclear into a couple of periods instead of bringing it on gradually. Wouldn't it be more realistic to hold the new nuclear capacity added in any single period to 8,000 MW, so no one round has to stand up an outsized program at once? What's pulling the model toward those lumpy nuclear builds?

sets.doc: the milestone decision years excluding the base year, in chronological order; these are the years at which capacity may be added and at which the system must be checked; a year counts as on-or-after another when it is at the same position or later in this ordered list

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the load blocks of the annual load-duration curve, listed from the highest-demand block to the lowest; 'peak' is the first block; a block is said to lie at-or-below another in this merit ordering when it appears at the same position or later in this list

sets.name: mh
sets.doc: the candidate hydro plants

sets.name: mt
sets.doc: the candidate thermal plant types

params.name: mdatah_initcap
params.doc: hydro plant capacity already in place at the start, in MW

params.name: mdatah_avail
params.doc: hydro plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatah_efact
params.doc: hydro plant energy factor, the fraction of installed capacity that bounds the plant's average sustained output over the year

params.name: mdatah_opcost
params.doc: hydro plant operating cost, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatah_capcost
params.doc: hydro plant capital cost, in million TL per MW of new capacity

params.name: mdatah_life
params.doc: hydro plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatah_maxcap
params.doc: the most new hydro capacity that may ever be built at a given hydro plant over the whole horizon, in MW

params.name: mdatat_initcap
params.doc: thermal capacity already in place at the start, in MW; this existing stock is treated as a vintage commissioned in the first decision year 1978

params.name: mdatat_avail
params.doc: thermal plant availability fraction, the share of installed capacity that can be counted on at any moment

params.name: mdatat_opcost
params.doc: thermal operating cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW-year of energy delivered

params.name: mdatat_opcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal operating cost; the operating cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_capcost
params.doc: thermal capital cost at base-year prices, in million TL per MW

params.name: mdatat_capcostg
params.doc: the annual growth rate applied to thermal capital cost; the capital cost of a vintage is its base cost compounded by this rate over the number of years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year

params.name: mdatat_life
params.doc: thermal plant economic life, in years

params.name: mdatat_maxcap
params.doc: the most new thermal capacity that may be built for a given type, in MW; unbounded for types with no listed limit

params.name: dd_duration
params.doc: the number of hours per year that the system spends in each load block

params.name: dd_demand
params.doc: the power demand of each load block in the base year 1975, in MW; the demand of any later year is this base demand grown by the demand growth rate over the number of years from 1975, rounded to the nearest MW

params.name: rho
params.doc: the discount (interest) rate per year

params.name: prr
params.doc: the planning reserve margin, the fraction of peak demand that available capacity must exceed

params.name: r
params.doc: the largest share of peak demand that aggregate hydro capacity is allowed to cover

params.name: g
params.doc: the annual demand growth rate

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted system cost over the horizon, in million TL

vars.name: phic
vars.doc: the capital charges incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: phio
vars.doc: the operating costs incurred in each milestone year, in million TL

vars.name: hh
vars.doc: new hydro capacity added at a given hydro plant in a given milestone year, in MW

vars.name: ht
vars.doc: new thermal capacity added for a given type in a given decision year, in MW; the year at which it is added is that vintage's commissioning year

vars.name: htt
vars.doc: total new thermal capacity built for a given type across the whole horizon, in MW

vars.name: zh
vars.doc: hydro power output of a given plant in a given load block in a given year, in MW

vars.name: zt
vars.doc: thermal power output indexed by type, then the vintage's commissioning year, then the load block, then the operating year, in MW; a vintage can only produce in years at or after its commissioning year

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every load block in every decision year, the total power produced across all generators must be enough to meet that block's demand. Because the load blocks are stacked from the heaviest block down, the production that serves a given block is the output of every plant in that block together with the output it carries in all the lighter blocks beneath it, and only generating vintages that have already been commissioned by that year may contribute. Demand for the year is the base-year demand of the block grown forward at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the system must hold enough firm capacity to cover peak demand with a reserve margin on top. Each plant's contribution is its availability fraction applied to the capacity it has in place that year, which is its starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, where the existing thermal stock counts as a first-year vintage. The sum of these firm contributions across all hydro and thermal plants must be at least the peak demand inflated by the reserve margin, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the plant cannot generate more than the capacity it actually has available. Adding up the plant's output over all load blocks in that year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times the capacity in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every thermal vintage in every decision year at or after the year that vintage was commissioned, the vintage cannot generate more than the capacity it has available. Adding up that vintage's output over all load blocks in the year, the total may not exceed its availability fraction times its installed capacity, which is the amount built for that vintage plus, for the very first vintage only, the pre-existing thermal stock of that type.

constraints.name: t);
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant in every decision year, the energy the plant delivers over the year is limited by its energy factor, not just its instantaneous capacity. Weighting the plant's output in each load block by the share of the year that the block and all lighter blocks below it occupy, the weighted total may not exceed the plant's energy factor times the capacity it has in place that year, namely its starting capacity plus all additions commissioned up to and including that year.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, hydro must not dominate the system. The combined capacity of all hydro plants in place that year, counting their starting capacity plus every addition commissioned up to and including that year, may not exceed an allowed share of that year's peak demand, with peak demand grown from the base year at the demand growth rate.

constraints.name: hh[mhh,
constraints.intent: For every hydro plant, the total of all new capacity built at that plant across all decision years may not exceed the most new capacity that plant is ever allowed to add.

constraints.name: htt[mtt]
constraints.intent: For every thermal type, the recorded total new capacity equals the sum of the capacity additions made for that type across all decision years.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the capital charges of that year are defined as the annualized cost of all capacity standing that year. For each hydro plant, take its capital recovery factor times its capital cost applied to the capacity it has commissioned up to and including that year. For each thermal type, take its capital recovery factor applied to its additions, with each vintage's addition valued at that vintage's escalated capital cost, which is the base capital cost compounded at its capital cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year, counting only vintages commissioned up to and including that year. The capital recovery factor spreads a plant's capital cost over its economic life at the discount rate. Set the year's capital charges equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: For every decision year, the operating costs of that year are defined from the energy each plant delivers. For each hydro plant, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at its operating cost. For each thermal vintage commissioned up to and including the year, value its duration-weighted output across the load blocks at that vintage's escalated operating cost, which is the base operating cost compounded at its operating cost growth rate over the years from the base year to the vintage's commissioning year. The duration weight of a block is the share of the year occupied by that block together with all lighter blocks below it. Set the year's operating costs equal to the sum of all these terms.

constraints.name: time)
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by bringing every year's costs back to present value. For each decision year, discount that year's capital charges and operating costs together by the discount factor for that year, which compounds the discount rate over the years from the base year. Set the total discounted cost equal to the sum of these discounted yearly costs over all decision years.

query: The 2005 round leans hard on both oil and nuclear at the same time, and that double-down worries me. Couldn't we just keep the new oil capacity added in 2005 under 12,000 MW and the new nuclear capacity added in 2005 under 25,000 MW together, instead of letting both run wide open in that final period? What's the tradeoff the model is weighing that pushes both up at once?
